text:
Corrected Overload Detection Formula
CPU Overload (from lib.rs:471-596)
cpu_pressure_overload = ALL 10 samples have (cpu_pressure > 80%)
dwell_latency_overload = ALL 10 samples have:
(read_dwell_per_ticket > 20,000 us)
OR
(write_dwell_per_ticket > 20,000 us)
CPU_OVERLOAD = cpu_pressure_overload AND dwell_latency_overload
What's NOT used for triggering (only for metrics):
- status_cmd_latency - tracked but doesn't trigger overload
- read_dwell_time.rate - tracked but doesn't trigger overload
- write_dwell_time.rate - tracked but doesn't trigger overload
Memory Overload (from memory_overload.rs: 315-372)
5 signals are evaluated each tick:
Signal
Condition
available_memory_last
available_memory_forecast
< 1024 MB
Predicts reaching O MB within forecast window
oom_score
≥ 1200
page_fault_rate
≥ 50,000/sec
Used in 2-of-3?
• Yes
• Yes
• Yes
X No (tracked only)
X No (tracked only)
some_memory_pressure_rate
≥ 50 per-mill
MEMORY_OVERLOAD = 2 of 3:
- available_memory_last < 1024 MB
available_memory_forecast predicts exhaustion
- oom_score >= 1200
Note: page_fault_rate and some_memory_pressure_rate are monitored and exported as metrics but do NOT contribute to the overload decision.
Final Formula
OVERLOAD_DETECTED = CPU_OVERLOAD OR MEMORY_OVERLOAD
Where:
CPU_OVERLOAD = (ALL 10 samples: cpu_pressure > 80%)
AND
(ALL 10 samples: read_dwell_per_ticket > 20ms OR write_dwell_per_ticket > 20ms)
MEMORY_OVERLOAD = 2 of 3:
[available_memory < 1024 MB]
[memory_forecast → 0 within 5s]
[oom_score >= 1200]

Why Some Signals Are "Metrics Only"?
Looking at the code, these signals are:
1.
Tracked - values recorded each tick
2.
Exported - available in FTDC/OTLP for analysis
3. Counted - overload counters increment when exceeded
But they don't trigger rate limiting. Likely reasons:
Still validating effectiveness (experimental)
Redundant with other signals
Too noisy / false positive prone
Reserved for future use
